MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff JOANNA L. PFEISTER (Tel: 617-429-2355) in pro per;
Atty. JOSEPH W. STRELLA, Esq. of Clapp, Moroney, Vucinich, Beeman and Scheley (Tel: 650-989-5400) for defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION's Motion For A Protective Order And Appointment Of Discovery Referee.

The Court notes this matter was not timely contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for a protective order is granted. Plaintiff opposes the motion on the ground that discovery is closed. By order filed November 23, 2021, however, the Presiding Judge continued the trial date to April 11, 2022, and ordered that all related deadlines, including discovery cutoff dates, are continued in accordance with the new trial date. The Court will enter a protective order providing that all depositions shall be conducted by videoconference. (See Cal. R. Ct. 3.1010.) Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

Defendant's motion for appointment of discovery referee is granted. The Court finds that it is necessary to appoint a referee to hear and determine any and all discovery motions and disputes relevant to discovery in the action due to the contentious nature of the parties' relationship and conduct to date, which has led to numerous contested discovery disputes and motions, as well as the need to provide continuing oversight of discovery by a single bench officer in order to reduce the likelihood of future such disputes and motions. If the parties are unable to agree on a referee, each party shall submit to the Court, no later than December 10, 2021, up to three nominees and the Court shall appoint a referee in accordance with the procedure set forth in Code of Civil Procedure section 640.

Order signed.
